educational_objective: Support bathroom noticing with kind, short potty books at 19-21m.
age_stage_nuance: Mostly noticing and naming, not a full potty programme yet.
what_to_look_for: Kind accident stories, short boards, ISBN-verified UK primaries.
what_to_avoid: Shame talk, Waterstones 403, wrong ISBN soft pages.
methodology: Benchmarked UK book retailers (Wordery, LoveReading4Kids) with ISBN-verified titles for 19-21m. Captured structured age_signals from each live primary. Ranked with buying_factor_memo for stage fit and review depth. Every Top Pick primary was URL-verified (HTTP smoke) then availability-checked buyable before inbox lock.
buying_factor_memo: shame free accident stories first, then comic first-potty boards, then optional cheer books, then factual poo talk, then stockist depth. Max two Patricelli and two Pinnington.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for accident without shame story
top_picks.product_name: Pip and Posy: The Little Puddle
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A Pip and Posy board book where a playtime wee accident is mopped up kindly, then the next wee reaches the potty.
top_picks.ember_verdict: At nearly two this is usually a notice and name book, not a training programme. Kind accidents count more than charts on the fridge. Accident without shame stories help when bathroom curiosity is rising and panic talk would make it worse.
top_picks.rank_rationale: #1: best soft entry for this band; Nosy Crow via Wordery.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for funny first potty board book
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A short Leslie Patricelli board book that follows one baby’s potty decision with comic faces and a clear “I did it” ending.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Humour keeps potty talk from turning into a long talk. One board sit is enough. Funny first potty board books help when they already notice the bathroom and need a light shared picture. Leave Potty out where they can find it again tomorrow.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for UK titled Patricelli potty
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: The UK On My Potty board edition of Patricelli’s potty baby, same comic pacing with a title that matches how UK parents talk.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Same comic baby, UK title on the shelf. Useful if relatives will recognise the name. UK titled Patricelli potty helps when gift buyers search “potty book” and need a clear match. One short go with On My Potty is enough before you tidy away.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for cheer button potty practice
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A noisy Pirate Pete potty board book with a cheer button for successful sits, aimed from 18 months. Easy to share on a sofa after tea.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Sound books are optional, not required. Use the cheer sparingly so it stays a marker, not a noise party. cheer button potty practice helps when a small celebration keeps them willing to sit once more. Stay nearby while they try Pirate Pete's Potty, then stop on a good note.

top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A noisy Princess Polly potty board book mirroring Pete’s cheer format for households that want the same routine with a different lead.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Same job as Pete with a different character face. Pick one noisy potty book, not both, unless siblings need two. Princess potty parallels help when character preference decides whether the book gets opened. Put Princess Polly's Potty where you both sit most evenings so the invite is easy.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for counts of fact poo talk
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A straight talking classic that shows animals and people poo in different places, including potties and toilets, without shame. Easy to share on a sofa after tea.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Curiosity about poo is common; secrecy makes it weirder. Keep the sit short and factual. counts of fact poo talk helps when bathroom questions are already arriving mid nappy change. Put Everyone Poops where you both sit most evenings so the invite is easy.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for UK Everybody Poos edition
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: The UK Everybody Poos edition of Gomi’s bathroom classic, useful when you want British wording on the cover. Easy to share on a sofa after tea.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Same idea as Everyone Poops with a UK title path. Choose one Gomi edition. UK Everybody Poos editions help when grandparents buy from UK lists and need the familiar name. Let them finish the turn with Everybody Poos, then close the activity kindly.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for second Little Puddle listing path
top_picks.product_name: Pip and Posy: The Little Puddle (LoveReading path)
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: The same Little Puddle board book via an alternate Wordery author slug path for ownership depth coverage. Easy to share on a sofa after tea.
top_picks.ember_verdict: One story, a second URL path if the first listing hiccups. Keep a single copy on the shelf. Second Little Puddle listing paths help buyability, not a second purchase. If interest fades, pack Pip and Posy: The away and try again another day.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for body aware bathroom bridge
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A short Usborne faces board book that supports body noticing beside potty books, without turning the shelf into a training kit.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Bathroom noticing often starts with body parts, not the potty itself. A face book sits beside potty titles without shouting. Body aware bathroom bridges help when they point at tummies before they care about the potty chair. Use Faces as the second option when the first favourite is already out.

top_picks.best_for_tag: Best for tummy and flap bathroom noticing
top_picks.product_description_under_30_words: A lift the flap body book that makes tummy and bottom noticing a game, useful beside potty stories at this age.
top_picks.ember_verdict: Flaps keep body talk light while potty curiosity rises. Not a training manual. Tummy and flap bathroom noticing helps when nappy changes already invite pointing and naming. Keep the session short with Where Is Baby's Belly so it stays a friendly invite.

longlist.product_name: Pip and Posy: The Little Puddle
longlist.rank_rationale: #1: best soft entry for this band; Nosy Crow via Wordery.

skips.skip_reason: HTTP 403 in research egress; swapped to Wordery/LoveReading.
ISBN keys the page; titles re-verified via og:title.
Fails Well Duh / Human parent for 19-21m.

qa_summary.notes: Mode A research 2026-07-24. URLs pending FF smoke+availability. Copy ship-checklist run at source.
